revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Bump]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "公卿" to "Kugyō"
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|天武天皇}}" to "|天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu}}"
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "天武天皇" to "Emperor Tenmu"
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Attempting to force efresh]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|宿禰}}" to "|宿禰|1=Sukune}}"
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Bump-01-18]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|八色の姓}}" to "|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}"
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12年2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.
