revision.comment: Text replacement - "2年" to "2nd year "
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13年12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "3年" to "3rd year "
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944年、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "4年" to "4th year "
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "7年" to "7th year "
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] on (CatAlot)
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Tier 2]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=draft}}. In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=draft}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=draft}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮|12=draft}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank|12=draft}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中|12=draft}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=draft}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|12=draft}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=draft}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史|12=draft}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参|12=draft}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=draft}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=draft}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|12=draft}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦|12=draft}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女|12=draft}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公|12=draft}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=draft}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=draft}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=draft}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|12=draft}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者|12=draft}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行|12=draft}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌|12=draft}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=draft}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=draft}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=draft}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮|12=draft}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown rank|12=draft}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中|12=draft}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=draft}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|12=draft}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=draft}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史|12=draft}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参|12=draft}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=draft}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=draft}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|12=draft}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦|12=draft}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女|12=draft}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公|12=draft}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=draft}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|12=draft}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人|12=draft}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=draft}}. His highest rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=draft}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|12=draft}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=draft}}. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.

His highest Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft|12=draft}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|12=draft|12=draft}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者|12=draft|12=draft}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行|12=draft|12=draft}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft|12=draft}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft|12=draft}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌|12=draft|12=draft}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=draft|12=draft}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=draft|12=draft}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|Rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=draft|12=draft}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft|12=draft}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft|12=draft}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft|12=draft}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=draft|12=draft}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史|12=draft|12=draft}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (Rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参|12=draft|12=draft}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft|12=draft}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft|12=draft}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=draft|12=draft}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|12=draft|12=draft}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公|12=draft|12=draft}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

His highest Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大伴 安麻呂 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[飛鳥時代]] - [[奈良時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = [[和銅]]7年[[5月1日 (旧暦)|5月1日]]（[[714年]][[6月21日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = 佐保大納言 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[正三位]][[大納言]]、[[贈位|贈]][[従二位]] | 主君 = [[天武天皇]]→[[持統天皇]]→[[文武天皇]]→[[元明天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴]][[連]]→[[宿禰]] | 父母 = 父：[[大伴長徳]] | 兄弟 = [[大伴御行|御行]]、'''安麻呂''' | 妻 = [[巨勢人]]娘・郎女、[[石川内命婦]] | 子 = [[大伴旅人|旅人]]、田主、[[大伴坂上郎女|坂上郎女]]、[[大伴稲公|稲公]]、[[大伴宿奈麻呂|宿奈麻呂]] | 特記事項 = }}
'''大伴 安麻呂'''（おおとも の やすまろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[公卿]]・[[歌人]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]のち[[宿禰]]。[[右大臣]]・[[大伴長徳]]の六男。[[官位]]は[[正三位]]・[[大納言]]、[[贈位|贈]][[従二位]]。'''佐保大納言'''と号す。
[[672年]]の[[壬申の乱]]では大海人皇子（[[天武天皇]]）の側に立ち、[[大伴吹負]]からの連絡の使者を務める。[[文武天皇|文武朝]]では兄・[[大伴御行|御行]]の没後に[[大伴氏]]の[[氏長者|氏上]]となり、[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年（[[702年]]）[[参議]]として公卿に列し、[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）には大納言に至った。
== 経歴 == === 天武・持統朝 === [[天智天皇|天智朝]]にて、[[巨勢人]]の娘・郎女を娉った[[和歌]]が『[[万葉集]]』に採録されている<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>。
[[天武天皇]]元年（[[672年]]）の6月に大海人皇子が近江の朝廷に対して兵を興すため東国に向かうと、倭（[[大和国|大和]]）の自邸にいた[[大伴吹負]]は皇子のために同志を募り、「一、二の族および諸豪傑」あわせて数十人を得た。「一、二の族」の中に、大伴安麻呂があったと思われる。吹負は29日に挙兵し、敵の内応によって倭京を奪取した。この成功を[[美濃国]]の不破宮に報じる使者として、大伴安麻呂・[[坂上老]]・[[佐味宿那麻呂]]が派遣された。彼らは無事に任務を果たしたらしい。宿那麻呂はこの後とって返して吹負の下で戦ったが、安麻呂のその後の行動は不明である。同様に吹負のもとに戻ったかもしれないが、別の方面にいたかもしれない。
天武天皇12年（[[684年]]）[[広瀬王]]と大伴安麻呂は[[判官]]・[[録事]]・[[陰陽師]]・[[工匠]]を引き連れて畿内を回り、都の候補地を探した（この時の[[七色十三階冠|冠位]]は[[小錦中]]）<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇12年2月28日条</ref>。天武天皇13年（[[685年]]）[[八色の姓]]の制定により[[大伴氏|大伴連]]など50氏が[[宿禰]]の姓を与えられており<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年12月2日条</ref>、安麻呂もこの時連姓から宿禰姓に[[改姓]]したとみられる。
[[朱鳥]]元年（[[686年]]）正月に[[新羅使]]・金智祥を接待するために、安麻呂は[[河内王#河内王 (飛鳥時代)|川内王]]・[[藤原大島]]・[[境部このしろ|境部鯯魚]]・[[穂積虫麻呂]]と共に筑紫に遣わされた（この時の冠位は[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|直広参]]）。同年9月9日に天武天皇が[[崩御]]すると、28日にその[[殯|殯宮]]で安麻呂は大蔵のことを誄した。持統天皇2年（[[688年]]）8月に[[天武天皇]]の殯宮で嘗して慟哭する儀式があったが、この時も安麻呂が誅を述べている<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇2年8月10日条</ref>。
=== 文武・元明朝 === [[文武天皇|文武朝]]初頭まで、大伴氏の[[氏長者|氏上]]は兄・[[大伴御行|御行]]であり、安麻呂が政治の中枢に加わることはなかった。しかし[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]）に御行が没すると、[[官位]]の上で安麻呂が大伴氏の最高位となり、翌年には朝政に参議するようになる。大宝元年（701年）3月21日に[[大宝律令|大宝令]]にもとづく[[位階]]が授けられた際、安麻呂は[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|直大壱]]から[[従三位|正従三位]]に進んだ。大宝2年（[[702年]]）正月17日に[[式部省|式部卿]]に任ぜられ、同年5月21日には[[粟田真人]]・[[高向麻呂]]・[[下毛野古麻呂]]・[[小野毛野]]と共に、朝政に参議することを命じられた。これが[[参議]]の始めで、このときは参議という[[官職]]に任命されたのではなく、「参議する」という仕事を命じられたのである。同年6月24日に[[兵部省|兵部卿]]を兼ねた。
以上は『[[続日本紀]]』によるもので、『[[公卿補任]]』は細部が異なる。大宝元年（701年）3月19日に従三位・中納言になり、21日に中納言をやめて[[散位]]になったとする。翌年に式部卿というのは同じだが、二箇所で1月7日と4月7日の異なる日付を記す。それから5月17日に参議になり、6月19日に兵部卿を兼ねたとする。
慶雲2年（[[705年]]）8月に[[大納言]]に任ぜられ、11月には[[大宰帥]]を兼ねる。その[[大宰府|大弐]]に[[石川宮麻呂]]が任命されている。
[[元明天皇|元明朝]]に入り、[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]）3月に大納言に任官する。大納言は3年前の慶雲2年（[[705年]]）にも任ぜられているが、再任された事情ははっきりしない。7月に元明天皇は[[議政官]]とそれ以外の高級官人とを分けてそれぞれに訓示を与えているが<ref>『続日本紀』和銅元年7月15日</ref>、安麻呂は前者のうちにあった。
和銅7年（[[714年]]）5月1日薨去。最終官位は大納言兼大将軍正三位。元明天皇は深く悼み、[[従二位]]を贈った。この際に[[鈴鹿王]]らが遣わされたという<ref name="kb" />。
== 永隆寺創建 == 永隆寺は大伴氏の[[氏寺]]で伴寺とも呼ばれた。[[平安時代]]に成立した『[[東大寺要録]]』には、永隆寺は大伴安麿が建立したものとある。それによれば飯高天皇（[[元正天皇]]）の世の[[養老]]2年（[[718年]]）に奈良坂東阿古屋谷に永隆寺が立ったとする<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊・編『東大寺要録』、1944年、全国書房、249頁。</ref>。しかし養老2年は安麻呂の死から数年の後である。
== 人物 == 万葉歌人として、[[和歌]]作品3首が『[[万葉集]]』に採録されている<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[六国史]]』による。 *[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]） 2月28日：見[[小錦中]]。12月2日：[[連]]姓から[[宿禰]]姓に改姓（[[八色の姓]]制定） *天武天皇15年（[[686年]]） 正月：見直広参 *時期不詳：直大壱 *[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]） 3月21日：正冠[[従三位]]<ref>『公卿補任』では3月19日に従三位、中納言。21日に停中納言為散位</ref> *大宝2年（[[702年]]） 正月17日：[[式部省|式部卿]]<ref>『公卿補任』では1月7日と4月7日</ref>。5月21日：令参議朝政。6月24日：[[兵部省|兵部卿]] *[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]） 4月20日：[[中納言]]<ref name="kb">『公卿補任』</ref>。8月11日：[[大納言]]<ref>『公卿補任』では8月1日</ref>。11月28日：兼[[大宰帥]]<ref>『公卿補任』では11月14日</ref> *[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]） 3月12日：[[正三位]]、辞大宰帥<ref name="kb" />。3月13日：大納言 *和銅7年（[[714年]]） 5月1日：薨去（大納言兼大将軍正三位）、[[贈位|贈]][[従二位]]
== 系譜 == *父：[[大伴長徳]]<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> *母：不詳 *妻：巨勢郎女 - [[巨勢人]]の娘 **長男：[[大伴旅人]]（665-731）<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> **男子：大伴田主 *妻：[[石川内命婦]] **女子：[[大伴坂上郎女]] - [[穂積親王]]妃、のち大伴宿奈麻呂室 **男子：[[大伴稲公]] *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[大伴宿奈麻呂]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *高島正人『奈良時代諸氏族の研究』、吉川弘文館、1983年、ISBN 4-642-02118-3。 *筒井英俊・編『東大寺要録』、全国書房、1944年。 *星野良作「壬申の乱と大伴連氏」、『壬申の乱研究の展開』（吉川弘文館、1997年）所収、ISBN 4-642-02316-X。 *[[宇治谷孟]]『日本書紀 （下）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1988年 *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の やすまろ}} [[Category:大伴氏|やすまろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:従二位受位者]] [[Category:万葉歌人]] [[Category:壬申の乱]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:714年没]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[大伴安麻呂]] to [[Otomo no Otomaro]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

== 系譜 == *父：[[大伴長徳]]<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7年5月1日条</ref> *母：不詳 *妻：巨勢郎女 - [[巨勢人]]の娘 **長男：[[大伴旅人]]（665-731）<ref>『続日本紀』天平3年7月25日条</ref> **男子：大伴田主 *妻：[[石川内命婦]] **女子：[[大伴坂上郎女]] - [[穂積親王]]妃、のち大伴宿奈麻呂室 **男子：[[大伴稲公]] *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[大伴宿奈麻呂]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *高島正人『奈良時代諸氏族の研究』、吉川弘文館、1983年、ISBN 4-642-02118-3。 *筒井英俊・編『東大寺要録』、全国書房、1944年。 *星野良作「壬申の乱と大伴連氏」、『壬申の乱研究の展開』（吉川弘文館、1997年）所収、ISBN 4-642-02316-X。 *[[宇治谷孟]]『日本書紀 （下）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1988年 *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の やすまろ}} [[Category:大伴氏|やすまろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:従二位受位者]] [[Category:万葉歌人]] [[Category:壬申の乱]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:714年没]]

revision.text:
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|12=draft}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人|12=draft}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}. His {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=draft}}. His highest Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=draft}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|12=draft}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|reign of Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者|12=draft}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行|12=draft}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|reign of Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|waka|ja|和歌|12=draft}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=draft}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=draft}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=draft}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮|12=draft}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|Rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=draft}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中|12=draft}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Monmu period|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=draft}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|12=draft}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=draft}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rokkokushi|ja|六国史|12=draft}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (Rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参|12=draft}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|12=draft}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=draft}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=draft}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|12=draft}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦|12=draft}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女|12=draft}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公|12=draft}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=draft}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.
{{translated page|ja|大伴安麻呂|version=93631194|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|qq=draft}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦|qq=draft}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女|qq=draft}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公|qq=draft}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|qq=draft}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.
