{{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = 1st day of the 5th lunar month of {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Wadō}} 7 (June 21, 714) | other_names = Saho Dainagon | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, posthumously Jushii | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} → {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} → {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}} → {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}} | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} (Muraji → Sukune) | parents = Father: Ōtomo no Nagatoku | siblings = {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikiko|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikiko|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}}, Yasumaro | spouse = Daughter of Kose no Hito, Ishikawa Naimefu | children = {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, Tanushi, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Inagimi|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inagimi|1=Inakimi Otomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432352}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}} }}
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|court noble|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court noble|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waka poet|qq=}} active during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} and {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. His family title ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}}) was initially Muraji and later Sukune. He was the sixth son of the {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Udaijin}} Ōtomo no Nagatoku. His final Rank was Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously awarded Jushii. He was also known as Saho Dainagon.
Yasumaro played an instrumental role in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}, aligning with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). During the reign of Emperor Monmu, Yasumaro assumed leadership of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikiko|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikiko|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}}'s death. He was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} in the second year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō era}} (702 CE) and reached the position of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in the second year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun}} (705 CE).
== Career == === Tenmu and Jitō Eras === During the reign of Emperor Tenchi, Yasumaro married a daughter of Kose no Hito, and waka composed by him is included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}.<ref>{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=2|poem=101,102}}</ref>
In the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} of 672 CE, Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama, serving as a messenger for {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}}. When Fukei raised troops in Yamato, Yasumaro was one of the "first and second clans" who joined. Following their success in taking the Yamato capital, Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakagami no Omi|ja|坂上老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakagami no Omi|1=Sakanoue no Okina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424810}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukunamaro|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Sukunamaro|1=Sami no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383080}}, reported the victory to Prince Ōama at his headquarters in Mino Province.
In 684 CE, Yasumaro and Prince Hirose led a team of officials, including yin-yang masters and craftsmen, to survey potential sites for a new capital (at this time, Yasumaro held the Rank of Shōkinkō).<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref> Following the implementation of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, Yasumaro's kabane changed from Muraji to Sukune.
In 686 CE, Yasumaro, along with other officials such as {{ill|Kawachi-no-Mikoto|ja|河内王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ōshima|1=Nakatomi no Ōshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830084}}, hosted Silla envoys.<ref>『日本書紀』朱鳥元年正月条</ref> He delivered a eulogy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral and later performed a lamentation ritual at the emperor's mausoleum in 688 CE.<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇2nd year 8月10日条</ref>
=== Monmu and Genmei Eras === Yasumaro ascended to political prominence following his brother Mikiko's death in 701 CE, becoming the clan's head and entering the council of ministers. He received the Rank of Shōkonsanmi during the reforms of the Taihō Code. By 702 CE, he had been appointed {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} and served as {{ill|Shikibu-shō|ja|式部卿|Shikibu-shō=Shikibu-shō|lt=Shikibu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-shō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}, later assuming the role of {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja|兵部卿|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|lt=Hyōbu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}.
In 705 CE, Yasumaro reached the Rank of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and was appointed {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}} later that year. During the Genmei era, Yasumaro was reappointed to the position of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in 708 CE.
Yasumaro passed away on the 1st day of the 5th lunar month of 714 CE. His final Rank was Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously awarded Jushii.
== Establishment of Eiryūji Temple == Eiryūji Temple, also known as Tomo-dera, was a {{ill|clan temple|ja|氏寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan temple|qq=}} of the Ōtomo clan. According to the {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Yōroku|qq=}}, it was founded by Yasumaro and completed in 718 CE, several years after his death.<ref>{{Cite book|title=東大寺要録|editor=Tsuchii Hideto|year=1944|publisher=全国書房|page=249}}</ref>
== Personal Life == Yasumaro was a waka poet with three of his works included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}.<ref>{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=2|poem=101|volume=3|poem=299|volume=4|poem=517}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * 684 CE: Appointed Shōkinkō; surname changed from Muraji to Sukune. * 686 CE: Appointed Shōkōsan. * 701 CE: Promoted to Shōkonsanmi; began duties as {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}. * 702 CE: Served as {{ill|Shikibu-shō|ja|式部卿|Shikibu-shō=Shikibu-shō|lt=Shikibu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-shō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja|兵部卿|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|lt=Hyōbu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}. * 705 CE: Appointed {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and later {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. * 714 CE: Passed away and posthumously awarded Jushii.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Eldest son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: Ishikawa Naimefu ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inagimi|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inagimi|1=Inakimi Otomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432352}} * Children of unknown mother: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}}
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Takashima Masato, *Research on Nara-Period Clans*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983, ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsuchii Hideto, *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino Ryōsaku, "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Jinshin War Research Compilation*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997, ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * {{ill|Ujitani Makoto|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Makoto|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 2)*, Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 1988. * {{ill|Ujitani Makoto|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Makoto|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1)*, Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 1992.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0714|6|21}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|poet|WD=Q49757}}|spouse={{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}}; {{ill|Q97378899|WD=Q97378899}}|children={{ill|Inakimi Otomo|WD=Q11432352}}; {{ill|Otomo no Sukunamaro|WD=Q10933541}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoko|WD=Q11432357}}|relatives={{ill|Ōtomo no Miyuki|WD=Q11432338}}|native_name=大伴安麻呂}} '''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/poet|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=kabane}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}. His highest Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=reign of Emperor Monmu}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan head|qq=}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikoshi|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=reign of Emperor Tenji}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=waka}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaue no Oshikoro|1=Sakanoue no Okina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424810}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Sukune|1=Sami no Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383080}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Palace|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|Rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinchū|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Monmu period}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Yōroku|qq=}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Rokkokushi}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (Rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūhironari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|lt=Ishikawa no Iratsume|lt=Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchinome|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inamaro|qq=}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|qq=}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

{{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = 1st day of the 5th lunar month of {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Wadō}} 7 (June 21, 714) | other_names = Saho Dainagon | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, posthumously Jushii | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} → {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} → {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}} → {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}} | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} (Muraji → Sukune) | parents = Father: Ōtomo no Nagatoku | siblings = {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikiko|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikiko|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}}, Yasumaro | spouse = Daughter of Kose no Hito, Ishikawa Naimefu | children = {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, Tanushi, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Inagimi|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inagimi|1=Inakimi Otomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432352}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}} }}
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|court noble|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court noble|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waka poet|qq=}} active during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} and {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. His family title ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}}) was initially Muraji and later Sukune. He was the sixth son of the {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Udaijin}} Ōtomo no Nagatoku. His final Rank was Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously awarded Jushii. He was also known as Saho Dainagon.
Yasumaro played an instrumental role in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}, aligning with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). During the reign of Emperor Monmu, Yasumaro assumed leadership of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikiko|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikiko|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}}'s death. He was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} in the second year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō era}} (702 CE) and reached the position of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in the second year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun}} (705 CE).
== Career == === Tenmu and Jitō Eras === During the reign of Emperor Tenchi, Yasumaro married a daughter of Kose no Hito, and waka composed by him is included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}.<ref>{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=2|poem=101,102}}</ref>
In the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} of 672 CE, Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama, serving as a messenger for {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}}. When Fukei raised troops in Yamato, Yasumaro was one of the "first and second clans" who joined. Following their success in taking the Yamato capital, Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakagami no Omi|ja|坂上老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakagami no Omi|1=Sakanoue no Okina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424810}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukunamaro|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Sukunamaro|1=Sami no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383080}}, reported the victory to Prince Ōama at his headquarters in Mino Province.
In 684 CE, Yasumaro and Prince Hirose led a team of officials, including yin-yang masters and craftsmen, to survey potential sites for a new capital (at this time, Yasumaro held the Rank of Shōkinkō).<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref> Following the implementation of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, Yasumaro's kabane changed from Muraji to Sukune.
In 686 CE, Yasumaro, along with other officials such as {{ill|Kawachi-no-Mikoto|ja|河内王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ōshima|1=Nakatomi no Ōshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830084}}, hosted Silla envoys.<ref>『日本書紀』朱鳥元年正月条</ref> He delivered a eulogy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral and later performed a lamentation ritual at the emperor's mausoleum in 688 CE.<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇2nd year 8月10日条</ref>
=== Monmu and Genmei Eras === Yasumaro ascended to political prominence following his brother Mikiko's death in 701 CE, becoming the clan's head and entering the council of ministers. He received the Rank of Shōkonsanmi during the reforms of the Taihō Code. By 702 CE, he had been appointed {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} and served as {{ill|Shikibu-shō|ja|式部卿|Shikibu-shō=Shikibu-shō|lt=Shikibu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-shō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}, later assuming the role of {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja|兵部卿|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|lt=Hyōbu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}.
In 705 CE, Yasumaro reached the Rank of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and was appointed {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}} later that year. During the Genmei era, Yasumaro was reappointed to the position of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in 708 CE.
Yasumaro passed away on the 1st day of the 5th lunar month of 714 CE. His final Rank was Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously awarded Jushii.
== Establishment of Eiryūji Temple == Eiryūji Temple, also known as Tomo-dera, was a {{ill|clan temple|ja|氏寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan temple|qq=}} of the Ōtomo clan. According to the {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Yōroku|qq=}}, it was founded by Yasumaro and completed in 718 CE, several years after his death.<ref>{{Cite book|title=東大寺要録|editor=Tsuchii Hideto|year=1944|publisher=全国書房|page=249}}</ref>
== Personal Life == Yasumaro was a waka poet with three of his works included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}.<ref>{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=2|poem=101|volume=3|poem=299|volume=4|poem=517}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * 684 CE: Appointed Shōkinkō; surname changed from Muraji to Sukune. * 686 CE: Appointed Shōkōsan. * 701 CE: Promoted to Shōkonsanmi; began duties as {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}. * 702 CE: Served as {{ill|Shikibu-shō|ja|式部卿|Shikibu-shō=Shikibu-shō|lt=Shikibu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-shō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja|兵部卿|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|lt=Hyōbu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}. * 705 CE: Appointed {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and later {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. * 714 CE: Passed away and posthumously awarded Jushii.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Eldest son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: Ishikawa Naimefu ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inagimi|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inagimi|1=Inakimi Otomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432352}} * Children of unknown mother: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}}
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Takashima Masato, *Research on Nara-Period Clans*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983, ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsuchii Hideto, *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino Ryōsaku, "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Jinshin War Research Compilation*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997, ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * {{ill|Ujitani Makoto|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Makoto|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 2)*, Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 1988. * {{ill|Ujitani Makoto|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Makoto|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1)*, Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 1992.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0714|6|21}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|poet|WD=Q49757}}|spouse={{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}}; {{ill|Q97378899|WD=Q97378899}}|children={{ill|Inakimi Otomo|WD=Q11432352}}; {{ill|Otomo no Sukunamaro|WD=Q10933541}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoko|WD=Q11432357}}|relatives={{ill|Ōtomo no Miyuki|WD=Q11432338}}|native_name=大伴安麻呂}} '''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/poet|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=kabane}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}. His highest Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=reign of Emperor Monmu}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan head|qq=}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikoshi|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=reign of Emperor Tenji}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=waka}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaue no Oshikoro|1=Sakanoue no Okina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424810}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Sukune|1=Sami no Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383080}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Palace|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|Rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinchū|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Monmu period}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Yōroku|qq=}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Rokkokushi}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (Rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūhironari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|lt=Ishikawa no Iratsume|lt=Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchinome|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inamaro|qq=}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|qq=}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

<!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--> ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox noble | name = Ōtomo no Yasumaro | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} - {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = 1st day of the 5th lunar month of {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Wadō}} 7 (June 21, 714) | other_names = Saho Dainagon | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, posthumously Jushii | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} → {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} → {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}} → {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}} | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} (Muraji → Sukune) | parents = Father: Ōtomo no Nagatoku | siblings = {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikiko|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikiko|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}}, Yasumaro | spouse = Daughter of Kose no Hito, Ishikawa Naimefu | children = {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, Tanushi, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Inagimi|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inagimi|1=Inakimi Otomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432352}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}} }}
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|court noble|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court noble|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waka poet|qq=}} active during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} and {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. His family title ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}}) was initially Muraji and later Sukune. He was the sixth son of the {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Udaijin}} Ōtomo no Nagatoku. His final Rank was Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously awarded Jushii. He was also known as Saho Dainagon.
Yasumaro played an instrumental role in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}, aligning with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). During the reign of Emperor Monmu, Yasumaro assumed leadership of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikiko|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikiko|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}}'s death. He was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} in the second year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō era}} (702 CE) and reached the position of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in the second year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun}} (705 CE).
== Career == === Tenmu and Jitō Eras === During the reign of Emperor Tenchi, Yasumaro married a daughter of Kose no Hito, and waka composed by him is included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}.<ref>{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=2|poem=101,102}}</ref>
In the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} of 672 CE, Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama, serving as a messenger for {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}}. When Fukei raised troops in Yamato, Yasumaro was one of the "first and second clans" who joined. Following their success in taking the Yamato capital, Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakagami no Omi|ja|坂上老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakagami no Omi|1=Sakanoue no Okina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424810}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukunamaro|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Sukunamaro|1=Sami no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383080}}, reported the victory to Prince Ōama at his headquarters in Mino Province.
In 684 CE, Yasumaro and Prince Hirose led a team of officials, including yin-yang masters and craftsmen, to survey potential sites for a new capital (at this time, Yasumaro held the Rank of Shōkinkō).<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref> Following the implementation of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, Yasumaro's kabane changed from Muraji to Sukune.
In 686 CE, Yasumaro, along with other officials such as {{ill|Kawachi-no-Mikoto|ja|河内王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ōshima|1=Nakatomi no Ōshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830084}}, hosted Silla envoys.<ref>『日本書紀』朱鳥元年正月条</ref> He delivered a eulogy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral and later performed a lamentation ritual at the emperor's mausoleum in 688 CE.<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇2nd year 8月10日条</ref>
=== Monmu and Genmei Eras === Yasumaro ascended to political prominence following his brother Mikiko's death in 701 CE, becoming the clan's head and entering the council of ministers. He received the Rank of Shōkonsanmi during the reforms of the Taihō Code. By 702 CE, he had been appointed {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} and served as {{ill|Shikibu-shō|ja|式部卿|Shikibu-shō=Shikibu-shō|lt=Shikibu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-shō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}, later assuming the role of {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja|兵部卿|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|lt=Hyōbu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}.
In 705 CE, Yasumaro reached the Rank of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and was appointed {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}} later that year. During the Genmei era, Yasumaro was reappointed to the position of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in 708 CE.
Yasumaro passed away on the 1st day of the 5th lunar month of 714 CE. His final Rank was Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously awarded Jushii.
== Establishment of Eiryūji Temple == Eiryūji Temple, also known as Tomo-dera, was a {{ill|clan temple|ja|氏寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan temple|qq=}} of the Ōtomo clan. According to the {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Yōroku|qq=}}, it was founded by Yasumaro and completed in 718 CE, several years after his death.<ref>{{Cite book|title=東大寺要録|editor=Tsuchii Hideto|year=1944|publisher=全国書房|page=249}}</ref>
== Personal Life == Yasumaro was a waka poet with three of his works included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}.<ref>{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=2|poem=101|volume=3|poem=299|volume=4|poem=517}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * 684 CE: Appointed Shōkinkō; surname changed from Muraji to Sukune. * 686 CE: Appointed Shōkōsan. * 701 CE: Promoted to Shōkonsanmi; began duties as {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}. * 702 CE: Served as {{ill|Shikibu-shō|ja|式部卿|Shikibu-shō=Shikibu-shō|lt=Shikibu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-shō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja|兵部卿|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|lt=Hyōbu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}. * 705 CE: Appointed {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and later {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. * 714 CE: Passed away and posthumously awarded Jushii.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Eldest son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: Ishikawa Naimefu ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inagimi|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inagimi|1=Inakimi Otomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432352}} * Children of unknown mother: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0714|6|21}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|poet|WD=Q49757}}|spouse={{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}}; {{ill|Q97378899|WD=Q97378899}}|children={{ill|Inakimi Otomo|WD=Q11432352}}; {{ill|Otomo no Sukunamaro|WD=Q10933541}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoko|WD=Q11432357}}|relatives={{ill|Ōtomo no Miyuki|WD=Q11432338}}|native_name=大伴安麻呂}} '''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/poet|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=kabane}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}. His highest Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=reign of Emperor Monmu}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan head|qq=}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikoshi|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=reign of Emperor Tenji}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=waka}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaue no Oshikoro|1=Sakanoue no Okina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424810}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Sukune|1=Sami no Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383080}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Palace|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|Rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinchū|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Monmu period}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Yōroku|qq=}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Rokkokushi}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (Rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūhironari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|lt=Ishikawa no Iratsume|lt=Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchinome|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inamaro|qq=}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|qq=}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.

[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] [[en:Otomo no Otomaro]]
<!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--><!--Otomo no Otomaro--> ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox noble | name = Ōtomo no Yasumaro | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} - {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = 1st day of the 5th lunar month of {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Wadō}} 7 (June 21, 714) | other_names = Saho Dainagon | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, posthumously Jushii | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} → {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} → {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}} → {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}} | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} (Muraji → Sukune) | parents = Father: Ōtomo no Nagatoku | siblings = {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikiko|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikiko|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}}, Yasumaro | spouse = Daughter of Kose no Hito, Ishikawa Naimefu | children = {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, Tanushi, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Inagimi|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inagimi|1=Inakimi Otomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432352}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}} }}
'''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|court noble|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court noble|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} and {{ill|waka poet|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waka poet|qq=}} active during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} and {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. His family title ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}}) was initially Muraji and later Sukune. He was the sixth son of the {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Udaijin}} Ōtomo no Nagatoku. His final Rank was Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously awarded Jushii. He was also known as Saho Dainagon.
Yasumaro played an instrumental role in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}, aligning with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). During the reign of Emperor Monmu, Yasumaro assumed leadership of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikiko|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikiko|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}}'s death. He was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} in the second year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō era}} (702 CE) and reached the position of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in the second year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun}} (705 CE).
== Career == === Tenmu and Jitō Eras === During the reign of Emperor Tenchi, Yasumaro married a daughter of Kose no Hito, and waka composed by him is included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}.<ref>{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=2|poem=101,102}}</ref>
In the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} of 672 CE, Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama, serving as a messenger for {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}}. When Fukei raised troops in Yamato, Yasumaro was one of the "first and second clans" who joined. Following their success in taking the Yamato capital, Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakagami no Omi|ja|坂上老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakagami no Omi|1=Sakanoue no Okina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424810}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukunamaro|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Sukunamaro|1=Sami no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383080}}, reported the victory to Prince Ōama at his headquarters in Mino Province.
In 684 CE, Yasumaro and Prince Hirose led a team of officials, including yin-yang masters and craftsmen, to survey potential sites for a new capital (at this time, Yasumaro held the Rank of Shōkinkō).<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref> Following the implementation of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, Yasumaro's kabane changed from Muraji to Sukune.
In 686 CE, Yasumaro, along with other officials such as {{ill|Kawachi-no-Mikoto|ja|河内王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakatomi no Ōshima|ja|中臣大島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ōshima|1=Nakatomi no Ōshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830084}}, hosted Silla envoys.<ref>『日本書紀』朱鳥元年正月条</ref> He delivered a eulogy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral and later performed a lamentation ritual at the emperor's mausoleum in 688 CE.<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇2nd year 8月10日条</ref>
=== Monmu and Genmei Eras === Yasumaro ascended to political prominence following his brother Mikiko's death in 701 CE, becoming the clan's head and entering the council of ministers. He received the Rank of Shōkonsanmi during the reforms of the Taihō Code. By 702 CE, he had been appointed {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} and served as {{ill|Shikibu-shō|ja|式部卿|Shikibu-shō=Shikibu-shō|lt=Shikibu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-shō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}, later assuming the role of {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja|兵部卿|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|lt=Hyōbu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}.
In 705 CE, Yasumaro reached the Rank of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and was appointed {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}} later that year. During the Genmei era, Yasumaro was reappointed to the position of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in 708 CE.
Yasumaro passed away on the 1st day of the 5th lunar month of 714 CE. His final Rank was Shōsanmi {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously awarded Jushii.
== Establishment of Eiryūji Temple == Eiryūji Temple, also known as Tomo-dera, was a {{ill|clan temple|ja|氏寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan temple|qq=}} of the Ōtomo clan. According to the {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Yōroku|qq=}}, it was founded by Yasumaro and completed in 718 CE, several years after his death.<ref>{{Cite book|title=東大寺要録|editor=Tsuchii Hideto|year=1944|publisher=全国書房|page=249}}</ref>
== Personal Life == Yasumaro was a waka poet with three of his works included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}.<ref>{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=2|poem=101|volume=3|poem=299|volume=4|poem=517}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * 684 CE: Appointed Shōkinkō; surname changed from Muraji to Sukune. * 686 CE: Appointed Shōkōsan. * 701 CE: Promoted to Shōkonsanmi; began duties as {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}. * 702 CE: Served as {{ill|Shikibu-shō|ja|式部卿|Shikibu-shō=Shikibu-shō|lt=Shikibu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-shō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja|兵部卿|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|lt=Hyōbu-kyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}. * 705 CE: Appointed {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and later {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. * 714 CE: Passed away and posthumously awarded Jushii.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Eldest son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: Ishikawa Naimefu ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inagimi|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inagimi|1=Inakimi Otomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432352}} * Children of unknown mother: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}}
== References ==

== Bibliography == * Takashima Masato, *Research on Nara-Period Clans*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983, ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsuchii Hideto, *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino Ryōsaku, "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Jinshin War Research Compilation*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997, ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * {{ill|Ujitani Makoto|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Makoto|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 2)*, Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 1988. * {{ill|Ujitani Makoto|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Makoto|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1)*, Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 1992.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴安麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0714|6|21}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|poet|WD=Q49757}}|spouse={{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}}; {{ill|Q97378899|WD=Q97378899}}|children={{ill|Inakimi Otomo|WD=Q11432352}}; {{ill|Otomo no Sukunamaro|WD=Q10933541}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287}}|father={{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoko|WD=Q11432357}}|relatives={{ill|Ōtomo no Miyuki|WD=Q11432338}}|native_name=大伴安麻呂}} '''Ōtomo no Yasumaro''' (大伴 安麻呂) was a {{ill|statesman|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/statesman|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} and {{ill|poet|ja|歌人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/poet|1=waka poet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11122954}} active from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=kabane}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, later changed to {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune}}. He was the sixth son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}. His highest Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and he was posthumously promoted to {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. He was also known as '''Saho Dainagon'''.
During the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}} of 672, he sided with Prince Ōama (later {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}), serving as a messenger under {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=reign of Emperor Monmu}} and later periods, he became the {{ill|clan head|ja|氏長者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan head|qq=}} of the Ōtomo clan after his elder brother {{ill|Ōtomo no Mikoshi|ja|大伴御行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Mikoshi|1=Ōtomo no Miyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432338}} passed away. He joined the Council of State in 702 as a {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} and was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} in 705.
== Career == === During the Reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō === During the {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=reign of Emperor Tenji}}, Yasumaro composed a {{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=waka}} for his bride, the daughter of {{ill|Kose no Hito|ja|巨勢人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Hito|1=Kose no Hito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479766}} (郎女), which was included in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,0102</ref>.
In the first year of Tenmu's reign (672), Yasumaro supported Prince Ōama in the Jinshin War. When {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}} gathered allies in Yamato for Prince Ōama, Yasumaro was one of his key supporters. Yasumaro, along with {{ill|Sakaue no Oshikoro|ja|坂上老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaue no Oshikoro|1=Sakanoue no Okina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424810}} and {{ill|Sami no Sukune|ja|佐味宿那麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sami no Sukune|1=Sami no Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383080}}, served as messengers to report military successes to Prince Ōama at the {{ill|Fuwa Palace|ja|不破宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Palace|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Mino Province. After the war, Yasumaro's subsequent actions are unclear, but he may have continued to assist Fukei or taken a different assignment.
In 684, Yasumaro, accompanied by Prince Hirose and others, traveled through the Kinai region to scout potential sites for the capital. This suggests that his {{ill|Rank|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinchū|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 2月28日条</ref>. Later that year, under the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}, the Ōtomo clan's kabane was changed from Muraji to Sukune<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref>.
In 686, Yasumaro was part of a delegation sent to Chikushi to host envoys from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}. Later that year, he delivered an elegy at Emperor Tenmu's funeral.
=== During the Reigns of Emperor Monmu and Empress Genmei === Until the early {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Monmu period}}, Yasumaro played a secondary role to his brother Mikoshi. However, upon Mikoshi's death in 701, Yasumaro assumed the leadership of the Ōtomo clan and rose in prominence. In 702, he was appointed {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}, beginning his active participation in the Council of State.
In 705, Yasumaro was promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}}, and later that year, he also served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}, Yasumaro retained his position as Dainagon until his death in 714.
== Establishment of Eiryū-ji == Eiryū-ji, the Ōtomo clan's temple, was reportedly founded by Yasumaro in 718, a few years after his death. The {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Yōroku|qq=}} attributes its construction to him<ref>『東大寺要録』巻6、末寺章第9。筒井英俊『東大寺要録』全国書房、1944th year 、249頁。</ref>.
== Legacy == As a poet, Yasumaro contributed three waka to the Man'yōshū<ref>『万葉集』巻02-0101,03-0299,04-0517</ref>.
== Official Titles == Notes are based on the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Rokkokushi}} unless otherwise stated. * Tenmu 13 (684): Appointed Shōkinchū; renamed to Sukune under the Yakusa no Kabane system. * 686: Appointed Chikushi envoy (Rank: {{ill|Chūhironari|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūhironari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * 701: Promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}. * 702: Appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}; served as Sangi. * 705: Promoted to {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and concurrently served as {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. * 714: Passed away as Dainagon and Senior Third Rank; posthumously awarded Junior Second Rank.
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Nagatoku|ja|大伴長徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Nagatoku|1=Ōtomo no Nagatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432357}}<ref>『続日本紀』和銅7th year 5月1日条</ref> * Wife: Daughter of Kose no Hito ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}} (665–731)<ref>『続日本紀』天平3rd year 7月25日条</ref> ** Son: Ōtomo no Tanushi * Wife: {{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|lt=Ishikawa no Iratsume|lt=Ishikawa no Uchinome|ja|石川内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchinome|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ** Daughter: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}} ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Inamaro|ja|大伴稲公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inamaro|qq=}} * Unknown: ** Son: {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|qq=}}
== References == * Takashima, Masato. *Research on Nara Period Clans.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1983. ISBN 4-642-02118-3. * Tsutsui, Eishun (ed.). *Tōdaiji Yōroku.* Zenkoku Shobō, 1944. * Hoshino, Ryōsaku. "The Jinshin War and the Ōtomo Clan," in *Studies on the Jinshin War.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. ISBN 4-642-02316-X. * Ujitani, Mō. *Nihon Shoki (Part II).* Kodansha, 1988. * Ujitani, Mō. *Shoku Nihongi (Part I).* Kodansha, 1992.
